summary: All 4 hypotheses confirmed in iteration 3. The closed-form predictor M_est = round(n_ITL + 3√n_ITL + 0.05·wait_budget) achieves max error of 2 units (better than predicted ≤5), and a predictor-guided binary search finds exact optimal MaxBatchSize in ≤5 solver calls — a 44% reduction from full binary search.
key_points: h-main CONFIRMED: Predictor achieves max_error=2 across all scenarios (baseline: 38 vs 40, long-loose-itl: 91 vs 93, small-queue: 38 vs 38). Two dominant components (n_ITL + 3√n_ITL) account for 99% of the estimate when wait_budget is small.

h-ablation CONFIRMED: Removing the 0.05·wait_budget term increases long-loose-itl error from 2 to 8 (increase of 6, meeting ≥6 threshold), while baseline and small-queue errors remain unchanged — proving the wait term is load-bearing only for large-wait-budget scenarios.

h-control-negative CONFIRMED: The sign of wait_budget perfectly separates crossover from no-crossover scenarios (short-tight-ttft has wait_budget=-11.29ms → no crossover; all 3 others positive → crossover). This provides a zero-cost classifier before any solver calls.

h-robustness CONFIRMED: Predictor-guided strategy achieves exact M* (gap=0) for all 3 crossover scenarios in ≤5 calls (small-queue in only 4). The no-crossover case lands on the throughput plateau with relative gap 1.3e-5 (~0%). Total reduction from 9 calls to 5 = 44% savings.
